meaning: adj.被咒语所镇住的,着迷的,出神的
examples: His audience had listened like children, spellbound by his words.
Entranced, he was silent for a long space, spellbound.
Yet for close on 90 minutes he held his audience spellbound.
He will doubtless keep the courtroom in The Hague spellbound with his oratory and choice phrases.
Shannon holds Greengrass spellbound with tales of his army life, but he is not all he seems.
